title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. text_content: Varian utilizes a process based on the Rational Unified Process, and is defined in document reference DND01 Design and Development. This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. A second document, RSK01 Medical Device Risk Management is used to supplement DND01 and provides guidance on Risk Management consistent with EN ISO 14971:2019 Error! Reference source not found.. title: 3.2.1 Scope, Schedule and Budget Control
text_content: Scope control will consist of managing requirement changes, enhancements, feature requests and scope changes.  Each will be controlled as outlined below. Requirement changes: If all of these conditions are met:  The changes do not impact the essential functionality and/or user experience of the device  The changed do not impact the program schedule committed to MER by more than two calendar weeks Then the changes will be managed through the document control process using Review Report Forms (LL40). If the above conditions are not all met, then the requirement change is elevated to a change in scope by definition.  See Scope Change section below. Enhancements and Feature Requests: Any requirement changes to implement additional features or enhancements, after Elaboration Phase, will be collected as TFS backlog items reporting to an Enhancement EPIC. Medsafe (NZ) are required to be notified only if there are changes to the information on the WAND database. Similar to the TGA, this includes information such as GMDN code, manufacturer’s details and the manufacturer’s Intended Purpose. Ethos Radiotherapy System 4.0: A variation request or new registration for Ethos Radiotherapy System 4.0 is not required to be submitted the TGA. Variations are only required when there is a substantial change to the design, intended performance or quality management system of the device. Legislation does not contain a definition for ‘substantial change’, however, at a minimum, changes to any details on the conformity assessment (CA) certificate constitute a substantial change. These include details such as GMDN code, manufacturer’s details and the manufacturer’s Intended Purpose.

text_content: Support & Maintenance Process Customers and any employee that experience erroneous behavior are advised to report these errors to the business unit that has sold the TPS system. These reports are analyzed by the respective complaint handling department. Every error is inserted into the corrective and preventive action (CAPA) and complaint handling procedures and, where necessary, is reproduced in the laboratory for analysis. The complaint handling and the CAPA procedures ensure that the complaints are assessed for the need of Medical Device Reporting (MDR). Also ensured are a root cause analysis of the erroneous behavior and the provision of appropriate change requests for fixing the error. Depending on the significance of the problem, required changes are made by returning to the appropriate development state and implementing appropriate fixes. The changes are then retested and re-evaluated and are either distributed as updates through standard service

Variations are only required when there is a substantial change to the design, intended performance or quality management system of the device. Legislation does not contain a definition for ‘substantial change’, however, at a minimum, changes to any details on the conformity assessment (CA) certificate constitute a substantial change. text_content: Varian utilizes a process based on the Rational Unified Process, and is defined in document reference DND01 Design and Development. This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. source: D40348 HAL 4.0 TSCL for SN 4 Release 4.00.0122 Rev14.pdf
title: 4.0 verification and validation testing.
text_content: Verification Testing Halcyon SN4 was built per Pre-production Phase I (Silver) DMR, and is maintained in the production equivalent configuration using Discrepancy Report (DR) Handling and Engineering Change (EC) and QNotes processes. Issues that are found during testing are logged as DRs, and the impact to verification and validation testing is assessed. This assessment defines whether the part needs to be replaced or reworked in the machines after implementation of the design change. This decision is documented in the DR. The hardware changes are implemented, and the final material dispositioning (per DR) is done via EC process. Upgrades are performed per the information in ECs and DR and are documented in QNotes system. The Device Parts List of the verification devices is captured at the time of build, and before verification TRR. Validation Testing Halcyon SN4 was upgraded to the final production equivalent configuration (defined by Gold DMR).

title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives.
